food_subgroup: Chocolate cakes and baked chocolate desserts

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for molten chocolate cake or soffiato; closest_parent 'cake food product' (FOODON_00002400) used as the nearest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Chocolate cake) is the closest available category; no specific code for molten chocolate cake exists in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps specifically to molten chocolate cake or soffiato dessert.

preparation_method: oven-baked at controlled temperature to retain liquid chocolate centre

serving.natural_language_portion: One restaurant-style individual soffiato typically weighs approximately 120 g, served warm in a ramekin or on a plate, often accompanied by vanilla ice cream or cream.

nutrient_source.title: Chocolate lava cake, homemade — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central NDB No. 18354 (Cake, chocolate, commercially prepared with chocolate frosting) and peer-reviewed recipe analysis for molten chocolate cake
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry exists for 'soffiato' or 'molten chocolate cake'. Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for rich chocolate cake (NDB 18354) and dark chocolate (NDB 19904), weighted to reflect the high dark-chocolate and butter content of a classic soffiato recipe (approximately 50% dark chocolate and butter by weight, 20% eggs, 15% sugar, 10% flour, 5% cocoa). Energy and macros are consistent with published recipe analyses in peer-reviewed food science literature for fondant au chocolat / coulant. Values represent a best-estimate per-100g composite.

gi_source.title: Chocolate cake with chocolate frosting — GI value from University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate applied to molten dark chocolate cake
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database reports GI values for chocolate cake in the range of 38–40 (testing reference: Foster-Powell et al., 2002, Am J Clin Nutr 76:5–56). Soffiato / molten chocolate cake has a similar macronutrient profile — high fat, moderate sugar, low flour content — which suppresses glycemic response. GI of 38 is applied as a defensible analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (38 × 50.4) / 100 ≈ 19.2, rounded to 19.0, based on 120 g serving with 42 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and high saturated fat load
hypertension.guidance: Soffiato contains approximately 120 mg sodium and 15 g saturated fat per 100 g; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one small serving and avoid frequent consumption, as the combined sodium and saturated fat burden may contribute to elevated blood pressure over time.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (30 g per 100 g) and moderate glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Despite a low GI of approximately 38 due to high fat content, soffiato delivers approximately 30 g of sugar and a glycemic load of ~19 per standard serving; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this as an occasional indulgence, monitor post-prandial glucose response, and account for the full carbohydrate content in meal planning.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (15 g/100 g), cholesterol (115 mg/100 g), and trans fat content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Soffiato is high in saturated fat and cholesterol, with a small amount of trans fat from butter; individuals with cardiovascular disease or elevated LDL cholesterol should limit consumption to rare occasions and consider smaller portions, as regular intake may adversely affect lipid profiles and cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Soffiato is an Italian-style molten dark chocolate cake, a rich baked dessert characterised by a fully set outer sponge and a warm, flowing liquid dark chocolate centre that is released when the cake is cut or broken open. The name 'soffiato' derives from the Italian word meaning 'puffed' or 'blown', reflecting the light, airy exterior that contrasts with the dense molten interior. It is closely related to the French fondant au chocolat and the coulant au chocolat popularised by chef Michel Bras. The dessert is prepared by combining high-quality dark chocolate (typically 60–70% cocoa solids) with butter, eggs, sugar, and a small quantity of plain flour, then baking in individual ramekins at a high temperature for a short time — typically 10–12 minutes — so that the centre remains liquid while the exterior sets. The result is an intensely chocolatey, bittersweet dessert with a fudgy, dense outer layer and a silky, flowing ganache-like core. Soffiato is typically served warm, often accompanied by vanilla ice cream, whipped cream, or a dusting of icing sugar. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at approximately 420 kcal per 100 g, with high fat (26 g, of which 15 g is saturated), moderate carbohydrate (42 g), and significant sugar (30 g). The high fat content suppresses glycemic response, yielding a low GI of approximately 38, though the glycemic load per standard restaurant serving (~120 g) is still moderate at approximately 19. The dish is a source of iron (from dark chocolate) and provides small amounts of calcium, magnesium, and B vitamins. It is not suitable for individuals with gluten or egg allergies. Clinically, it should be consumed in moderation by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its high saturated fat, sugar, and caloric density. In Singapore, soffiato appears on the menu at Italian restaurants such as Ricciotti, where it is positioned as a premium dessert item.

Is soffiato the same as lava cake?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for 'soffiato' or 'molten chocolate cake' exists in any approved primary regional source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA) or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for rich chocolate cake and dark chocolate, weighted to reflect the known recipe composition of a classic soffiato. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for chocolate cake. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Food group corrected from 'Grains and staples' (as in input) to 'Desserts', which is the accurate classification for a baked chocolate dessert. 2. Nutrient values are estimated composites derived from USDA FoodData Central (NDB 18354 chocolate cake; NDB 19904 dark chocolate) weighted to reflect soffiato recipe proportions; no exact USDA or regional database entry exists for this dish. 3. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; this is confirmed zero as dark chocolate cake contains negligible ascorbic acid — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central data for chocolate cake. 4. GI of 38 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for chocolate cake (Foster-Powell et al., 2002); the high fat content of soffiato is expected to maintain or lower this GI relative to standard chocolate cake. 5. Monounsaturated_fat_g (-1 sentinel not used; value estimated at 7.5 g from USDA dark chocolate and butter composite). 6. Trans_fat_g is estimated at 0.2 g from butter content; this is a low but non-zero value consistent with USDA data for butter-containing baked goods. 7. The input food_group 'Grains and staples' appears to be a data entry error in the seed record; soffiato is unambiguously a dessert. 8. Halal status is marked false as restaurant-level halal certification for Ricciotti - The Riverwalk is not confirmed in public records; this should be verified with the restaurant directly. 9. All -1 values in per_100g represent nutrients not individually reported in the USDA composite source used for estimation and are documented as such per G3 sentinel value policy — however, in this record all micronutrients have been estimated from the USDA composite and no -1 values remain in the final output.
